China Guide / Human Resources and Payroll / Social Insurance

China Employee Termination in 2026: Pulse Risk-Register Brief for Foreign Employers

Key takeaways

  • Use a live risk register to manage China termination decisions, not static checklists.
  • Lead with legal route clarity, then control evidence, communication, payroll, and closure risk.
  • Route/evidence/payroll misalignment remains the highest-value intervention zone.

Plain-language legal context first

China is not an at-will termination system. Employer-initiated termination generally requires a lawful statutory basis under the Labor Contract Law framework, supported by coherent evidence and defensible process execution. Core anchors: Labor Contract Law, Decree No. 535 implementation rules, and SPC labor-dispute interpretation.

Pulse risk register (current)

Risk IDRisk statementLikelihoodImpactOwnerMitigation actionTrigger to escalate
RR-01Route rationale inconsistency across teamsMediumCriticalLegal + HR leadFreeze outward action and issue canonical route memoAny employee-facing step pending
RR-02Evidence chronology conflict or gapsMediumCriticalHR Ops + ComplianceReconcile versions and timeline before progressionUnresolved contradiction >24h
RR-03Script drift in manager communicationMediumHighHRBP + Manager leadRe-brief script and log acknowledgementRepeated off-script wording
RR-04Settlement/payroll/tax mapping mismatchMediumCriticalPayroll lead + LegalPre-lock line-item reconciliation and co-signPayroll lock window approaching
RR-05Local interpretation uncertaintyMediumHighLocal counsel + ComplianceComplete city-level validation memoFinal action date set
RR-06Closure evidence packet incompleteMediumMedium-HighOps + ComplianceKeep case open until archive completePost-payment closure request

Why this helps foreign employers

A risk register gives cross-border teams one clear operating view: what is unresolved, who owns it, and which item can block execution. This improves decision quality for leaders unfamiliar with local procedure detail.

What improved vs previous run

Compared with signal-ledger framing, this run improves prioritization discipline by adding likelihood/impact fields and explicit escalation triggers per risk row.

Minimum documentation controls

  • Canonical route memo
  • Versioned chronology log
  • Script acknowledgement record
  • Settlement mapping sheet
  • Payroll/tax reconciliation sheet
  • City-level validation memo
  • Closure evidence packet

Legal depth appendix (secondary)

This brief is grounded in Labor Contract Law, Decree No. 535, and SPC labor-dispute interpretation, with practical context from China Briefing, Littler, ICLG, and PwC.

Disclaimer

Informational only; not legal/tax/employment advice.

Sources

  1. http://www.npc.gov.cn/zgrdw/englishnpc/Law/2007-12/13/content_1384064.htm
  2. https://www.gov.cn/gongbao/content/2008/content_1124615.htm
  3. https://www.court.gov.cn/fabu/xiangqing/243981.html
  4. https://www.china-briefing.com/news/employee-termination-in-china/
  5. https://www.littler.com/news-analysis/asap/key-recent-developments-chinas-employment-law-china-us-comparative-perspective
  6. https://taxsummaries.pwc.com/peoples-republic-of-china/individual/taxes-on-personal-income
  7. https://iclg.com/practice-areas/employment-and-labour-laws-and-regulations/china
